text: In C, what is the primary role of the `mutable` keyword?
options: It allows a variable to be modified concurrently by multiple threads.
It allows a struct member to be modified even if the struct variable is declared as `const`.
It prevents a pointer from decaying into an array.
The `mutable` keyword does not exist in the standard C language; it is a C++ feature.
sizeof(struct Test) will be exactly 6 bytes.
sizeof(struct Test) will typically be 12 bytes due to padding.
sizeof(struct Test) will typically be 8 bytes.
The struct elements are reordered automatically by the compiler to use 6 bytes.

text: What is the correct syntax to declare a pointer to a function that takes an `int` as an argument and returns a `float`?
What does the bitwise expression `x ^ y ^ x` evaluate to, assuming `x` and `y` are integers?
In C++, why is it critical to make a base class destructor `virtual` when the class is intended to be inherited?
options: To prevent the instantiation of the base class.
To ensure that the derived class's destructor is called when an object is deleted through a base class pointer, preventing memory leaks.
To allow the destructor to be overloaded with different parameters.
To automatically initialize virtual base tables (V-Tables) for the derived objects.

text: Which of the following scenarios triggers a C++ Copy Constructor rather than an Assignment Operator?
options: MyClass obj1; MyClass obj2; obj2 = obj1;

text: In the context of C++ Polymorphism, early binding (static binding) is resolved by the compiler at compile-time. Which feature relies on late binding (dynamic binding)?
options: Virtual Functions overridden in a derived class

text: When resolving the 'Diamond Problem' (multiple paths of inheritance to the same base class) in C++, which keyword is used to ensure only one instance of the base class is inherited?
What makes a C++ class an 'Abstract Class'?
options: It contains at least one pure virtual function (e.g., `virtual void func() = 0;`).
All of its constructors are declared in the `private` access modifier section.
It cannot be inherited by any other class.

text: If you throw an exception of type `int` in C++ and do not have a specific `catch (int e)` block, what will happen if you have a `catch (...)` block?
options: The program will immediately crash with a 'Unhandled Exception' error.
The `catch (...)` block acts as a catch-all handler and will catch the integer exception.
A compilation error will occur because `catch (...)` is invalid syntax.
The integer is automatically converted to a string and caught by a `catch(string)` block if present.

text: In an AVL Tree, what is the strict property regarding the balancing factor of any node?
options: The height of the left and right subtrees must be exactly equal.
The absolute difference in heights of the left and right subtrees must not exceed 1.
The root node must always have a balance factor of 0.
The number of nodes in the left subtree must equal the number of nodes in the right subtree.

text: Which fundamental Data Structure is heavily utilized internally by the OS to evaluate arithmetic expressions written in Postfix (Reverse Polish) notation?
Which sorting algorithm provides a guaranteed worst-case time complexity of O(n log n)?
Performing an In-Order traversal on a valid Binary Search Tree (BST) will process the node values in which sequence?
In the context of Hashing, 'Linear Probing' is a technique used specifically to solve which problem?
If you are implementing a Priority Queue, which underlying data structure provides the most optimal time complexity for both insertion and extraction of the highest-priority element?
In Operating Systems, what is the primary purpose of Dijkstra's Banker's Algorithm?
Which CPU scheduling algorithm severely suffers from the 'Convoy Effect', where short processes get stuck waiting for a long process to release the CPU?
Belady’s Anomaly specifically occurs in which of the following Page Replacement Algorithms?
In OS synchronization, what happens when a process executes the `wait()` (or `P()`) operation on a Semaphore that currently has a value of 0?
options: The value of the semaphore becomes -1, and the process continues.
The process is blocked/suspended and placed in a waiting queue until the semaphore value becomes greater than 0.
The OS terminates the process immediately to prevent a deadlock.
The semaphore is automatically reset to 1.

text: What is 'Thrashing' in the context of Virtual Memory management?
options: When the CPU operates at maximum capacity, generating excess heat.
When the OS spends significantly more time swapping pages in and out of the disk than executing actual instructions.
When memory becomes so fragmented that no new processes can be loaded.
When a process accesses memory locations outside its designated address space.

text: In Database Normalization, a relation is formally defined to be in Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) if and only if:
options: It is in 3NF and has no multi-valued dependencies.
For every non-trivial functional dependency X → Y, X is a superkey.
Every non-prime attribute is fully functionally dependent on the primary key.

text: What is the fundamental difference between the `WHERE` clause and the `HAVING` clause in SQL?
options: `WHERE` filters rows before aggregate functions are applied; `HAVING` filters groups after aggregate functions are applied.
`HAVING` can only be used with subqueries, while `WHERE` is for standard queries.
`WHERE` is used in `SELECT` statements; `HAVING` is used in `UPDATE` statements.
There is no difference; they can be used interchangeably.

text: In the ACID properties of database transactions, what does 'Durability' guarantee?
options: That a transaction is treated as an indivisible unit.
That concurrent execution of transactions leaves the database in the same state as sequential execution.
That once a transaction is committed, its changes are permanently recorded in the database, even in the event of a system crash.
That only valid data matching table constraints is saved.

text: In an Entity-Relationship (ER) diagram, how is a 'Weak Entity' standardly represented?
options: By an ellipse with a dotted line.

text: Which fundamental data structure is utilized by modern relational databases (like MySQL/PostgreSQL) to implement primary indexes, because it stores data pointers strictly in linked leaf nodes, making range queries exceptionally fast?
If a file has the Unix permissions `-rwxr-xr--`, what is the equivalent numeric representation used in the `chmod` command?
Which Unix command is specifically utilized to search for a specific text pattern or regular expression within a file?
What does the COCOMO model primarily estimate in Software Engineering?
options: The number of expected bugs post-release

text: In Machine Learning, a model that performs exceptionally well on its training data but performs terribly on unseen test data is exhibiting which phenomenon?
Which Transport Layer protocol is connectionless, does not guarantee delivery, and has virtually no overhead, making it ideal for real-time video streaming or online gaming?
What is the primary difference between the `malloc()` and `calloc()` functions in C?
options: `malloc()` allocates contiguous memory, whereas `calloc()` allocates non-contiguous memory.
`malloc()` initializes the allocated memory to zero, whereas `calloc()` leaves it uninitialized.
`calloc()` takes two arguments (number of elements and size of each) and initializes the memory to zero, whereas `malloc()` takes one argument and leaves memory uninitialized.
`calloc()` is used strictly for arrays, while `malloc()` is used strictly for single structures.
Swaps the values of `a` and `b` without using a temporary third variable.
Calculates the bitwise NOR of `a` and `b`.
Results in a compilation error due to sequence point violations.

text: If `int arr[3][4];` is declared, what does the expression `*(arr + 1) + 2` represent?
What is the function of `sprintf()` in C?
options: To read formatted input from a string.
To print formatted output to the console screen.
To store formatted output into a character array (string) instead of printing it to the console.
To open a file and write a formatted string into it.

text: In C, if a global variable is declared with the `static` keyword, what effect does it have?
options: It prevents the variable from being modified.
It limits the scope of the global variable strictly to the file in which it is declared (internal linkage).
It stores the variable in the CPU register for faster access.
It makes the variable accessible from any file in the program.

text: In C++, what is the size of an empty class (a class with no member variables and no virtual functions) when instantiated using standard compilers?
options: Depends entirely on the operating system.

text: Which of the following statements about C++ `static` member functions is TRUE?
options: They can seamlessly access non-static member variables of the class.
They have a hidden `this` pointer passed to them automatically.
They can be declared as `virtual` to support runtime polymorphism.
They can only directly access other static members of the class and can be called without instantiating an object.

text: What happens if you attempt to invoke a `virtual` function from inside a C++ constructor?
options: A compilation error occurs because the Virtual Table (V-Table) is not yet built.
The program crashes with a Segmentation Fault.
Dynamic dispatch is disabled; the constructor calls the version of the function defined in its own class, not the derived class.
The function behaves exactly as standard runtime polymorphism, calling the most derived version.

text: In C++, the `explicit` keyword is placed in front of a constructor declaration primarily to prevent:
options: Implicit type conversions and copy-initialization that utilize that specific constructor.
The constructor from allocating dynamic memory.

text: Which C++ casting operator is specifically designed to safely downcast a base class pointer to a derived class pointer, performing a runtime check and returning `nullptr` if the cast is invalid?
Which type of C++ inheritance explicitly hides the base class's `public` members, making them `private` in the derived class?
In a strict Binary Tree of height `H` (where a single node tree has a height of 0), what is the formula for the MAXIMUM number of nodes the tree can contain?
What is the optimal time complexity to build a Binary Max-Heap from an unsorted array of `N` elements using the standard bottom-up heapify method?
Which property correctly defines the 'Load Factor' (λ) in a Hash Table?
options: The maximum number of collisions permitted before the program crashes.
The ratio of the number of elements stored (N) to the size of the hash table (M).
The prime number utilized in the hash function.
The depth of the deepest Linked List in a chaining implementation.

text: If Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm (Tortoise and Hare) detects a loop in a Linked List, how can you mathematically find the starting node of the loop?
options: Reset both pointers to the head and move them at the same speed.
Keep the fast pointer at the meeting point, reset the slow pointer to the head, and move both at a speed of 1 step per iteration until they meet.
Reverse the linked list from the meeting point.
It is mathematically impossible to find the start of the loop without utilizing a Hash Map.

text: Which of the following sorting algorithms is NOT an 'in-place' sort and requires O(N) auxiliary space?
What is the minimum number of edges required to create a fully connected undirected graph containing 'V' vertices?
In Operating Systems memory management, what is the fundamental difference between Paging and Segmentation?
options: Paging suffers from External Fragmentation, while Segmentation suffers from Internal Fragmentation.
Paging divides memory into fixed-size blocks, while Segmentation divides memory into variable-size blocks based on logical program divisions.
Paging requires a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB), while Segmentation does not.
Paging is entirely managed by the programmer, while Segmentation is managed by the hardware.

text: Which CPU Scheduling Algorithm fundamentally calculates a dynamically changing priority ratio `(Wait Time + Burst Time) / Burst Time` to prevent process starvation?
In the context of Deadlocks, a system is formally considered to be in a 'Safe State' if:
options: No process is currently requesting any resources.
The system has more available resources than the total maximum demand of all processes combined.
There exists at least one sequence of all processes such that each process can complete using currently available resources plus resources held by previously completed processes.
There are zero preemptible resources in the system.

text: Which statement accurately describes a key difference between a Mutex and a Binary Semaphore?
options: A Binary Semaphore can hold values up to 10, whereas a Mutex is strictly 0 or 1.
A Mutex implies an 'ownership' property where only the thread that locked the mutex can unlock it, whereas a Semaphore can be signaled by any thread.
Mutexes are utilized solely for Inter-Process Communication (IPC) across network sockets.
There is absolutely no functional or conceptual difference between them.

text: In Disk Scheduling algorithms, what does the SCAN algorithm do?
options: Services requests in the exact order they arrive (FCFS).
Moves the disk arm starting from the current position toward one end, servicing requests, and upon reaching the end, reverses direction and services requests on the way back.
Always services the request closest to the current head position (SSTF).
Services requests in a circular queue format, jumping back to the beginning without servicing requests on the return trip (C-SCAN).

text: Why do relational databases overwhelmingly prefer B+ Trees over standard B Trees for implementing indexes?
options: B+ Trees have a lower maximum height than B Trees.
B+ Trees store raw data records in the internal nodes, reducing the total node count.
B+ Trees store all data pointers exclusively in the leaf nodes, which are linked together, making sequential range queries significantly faster and more efficient.
B Trees do not support standard SQL operations.

text: Consider two tables, `A` and `B`. If you execute a `LEFT OUTER JOIN` on `A` and `B`, what will the result contain?
options: Only the rows that have matching keys in both tables.
All rows from table `B`, and the matched rows from table `A`.
All rows from table `A`, with NULL values filled in for columns from `B` where there is no matching key.
A full Cartesian product of `A` and `B`.

text: During Database Normalization, a decomposition of relation R into R1 and R2 is considered a 'Lossless-Join Decomposition' if and only if the intersection of R1 and R2 attributes is:
options: A primary key for the original relation R.
A candidate key or superkey for at least one of the decomposed relations (R1 or R2).

text: In transaction concurrency control, two operations are considered 'Conflicting' if they satisfy which three conditions?
options: They belong to different transactions, they access the same data item, and at least one is a Write operation.
They belong to the same transaction, they access different data items, and both are Read operations.
They belong to different transactions, they access the same data item, and both are Read operations.
They happen simultaneously on different CPU cores.

text: If a Relational Algebra Cartesian Product is performed between Relation X (10 rows, 4 columns) and Relation Y (5 rows, 3 columns), what is the resulting cardinality (rows) and degree (columns)?
Which specific flag is used with the Unix `grep` command to perform an 'inverted match' (i.e., display all lines that do NOT contain the specified pattern)?
What is the primary operational difference between Alpha Testing and Beta Testing in the Software Development Life Cycle?
options: Alpha testing is White-Box; Beta testing is purely automated.
Alpha testing is performed by actual end-users in a real-world environment; Beta testing is performed by developers.
Alpha testing is conducted at the developer's site by internal staff; Beta testing is conducted by real end-users at their own locations.
There is no difference; they are synonymous terms in Agile.

text: What is a 'Friend Class' in C++?
options: A class that inherits all properties of another class without using the `public` keyword.
A class whose member functions have access to the private and protected members of another class in which it is declared as a friend.
A class that cannot be instantiated and is used purely as an interface.
A class that is dynamically linked at runtime.

text: Which algorithm is highly optimized for finding the Shortest Path from a single source node to all other nodes in a graph that contains NON-NEGATIVE edge weights?
In the Unix/Linux file system, what information does an 'inode' (index node) specifically NOT contain?
options: The actual human-readable Name of the file

text: What does the `fseek()` function's parameter `SEEK_END` signify when adjusting a file pointer in C?
options: It seeks to the beginning of the file.
It seeks a specified number of bytes relative to the current position of the pointer.
It seeks a specified number of bytes relative to the end of the file.
It permanently truncates the file at the current pointer location.

text: According to Cayley's Formula, how many distinct spanning trees can be constructed from a completely connected undirected graph having 'n' vertices?
In a paged memory management system, what is the primary purpose of the Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
options: To store frequently accessed pages of data to prevent disk I/O.
To cache recent virtual-to-physical address translations to reduce the time taken to access the page table in main memory.
To prevent unauthorized access to restricted memory segments.
To translate high-level programming instructions into machine code.

text: What is the primary difference in meaning between the C declarations `int *p[5];` and `int (*p)[5];`?
options: They are identical in functionality; the parentheses are purely stylistic.
The first is an array of 5 integer pointers; the second is a single pointer to an array of 5 integers.
The first is a single pointer to an array of 5 integers; the second is an array of 5 integer pointers.
The first allocates memory dynamically; the second allocates memory statically.

text: In Relational Algebra, which operation is most appropriate to solve queries that include the phrase "for all" (e.g., "Find the students who have taken ALL courses offered by the CS department")?
In C++, what is the expected behavior if an exception is thrown from inside a destructor while another exception is already actively propagating (stack unwinding is in progress)?
options: The new exception replaces the old one and propagates upward.
Both exceptions are caught by the nearest `catch(...)` block.
The program immediately invokes `std::terminate()` and crashes.
The destructor simply returns early without throwing the second exception.

text: If a cache memory has a hit rate of 90%, a cache access time of 10ns, and a main memory access time of 100ns, what is the Effective Access Time (EAT) of the system? (Assuming simultaneous access is not used).
In the Unix file system, how does a 'Hard Link' conceptually differ from a 'Soft (Symbolic) Link'?
options: A hard link points to the target file's path, while a soft link points to the target file's inode.
A hard link points directly to the target file's inode, meaning the file data remains accessible even if the original file name is deleted.
Hard links can span across different file systems, whereas soft links cannot.
Hard links can be created for directories, whereas soft links cannot.

text: What does the standard C library function `scanf()` return upon successful execution?
options: The actual input string read from the user.
The memory address where the input data was stored.
The total number of input items successfully matched and assigned.
A boolean value (1 for success, 0 for failure).

text: In graph theory algorithms, a 'Minimum Spanning Tree' (MST) of a weighted, connected, undirected graph MUST include which of the following?
options: The edge with the absolute maximum weight in the entire graph.
The edge with the absolute minimum weight in the entire graph.

text: Which of the following is a known disadvantage of normalizing a database relation into Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) as opposed to stopping at 3rd Normal Form (3NF)?
options: BCNF may introduce new data anomalies.
BCNF does not necessarily preserve all original functional dependencies upon decomposition.
BCNF requires the use of multi-valued dependencies, which slows down queries.
BCNF tables cannot be joined using standard SQL.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following operations explicitly result in 'Undefined Behavior' (UB) according to the C standard?
options: Accessing an array outside of its declared bounds.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following statements regarding Constructors and Destructors in C++ are TRUE?
options: A constructor can be declared as `virtual`.
A destructor can be declared as `virtual`.
An abstract class can have a constructor.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following statements are mathematically TRUE for a standard Binary Search Tree (BST)?
options: An in-order traversal of a BST always visits the nodes in sorted (ascending) order.
The worst-case time complexity for searching a BST is O(N).
The left child of a node is always strictly less than the node, and the right child is strictly greater than or equal to the node.
A BST is always perfectly height-balanced by default upon insertion.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following mechanisms can an Operating System use to PREVENT Deadlocks? (Targeting the four necessary conditions).
options: Denying the 'Hold and Wait' condition by requiring a process to request all its resources simultaneously.
Allowing resource Preemption, forcibly taking resources away from blocked processes.
Using Dijkstra's Banker's Algorithm to track resource allocations dynamically.
Imposing a strict total ordering of all resource types to prevent 'Circular Wait'.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] In DBMS Concurrency Control, the Strict Two-Phase Locking (Strict 2PL) protocol provides which of the following guarantees?
options: It completely prevents Deadlocks from occurring.
It ensures that all resulting schedules are Conflict Serializable.
It prevents Cascading Rollbacks by holding exclusive locks until transaction commit/abort.
It eliminates the need for maintaining a transaction log.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Identify the valid memory allocation functions in C that return a `void*` pointer.
[MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following are valid characteristics of a `friend` function in C++?
options: It is NOT a member function of the class it is befriended to.
It can access `private` and `protected` data members of the class directly using an object reference.
A `friend` function is inherited by derived classes automatically.
It does not have a hidden `this` pointer passed to it.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] A Stack data structure is heavily utilized as the underlying mechanism for which of the following operations?
options: Executing Breadth-First Search (BFS) on a graph.
Managing function calls and recursion within a program.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] When multiple threads are spawned within the SAME process, which of the following memory/OS resources are SHARED among all those threads?

options: The expression `NULL = NULL` evaluates to TRUE.
The aggregate function `COUNT(*)` includes rows containing NULL values.
The aggregate function `COUNT(column_name)` ignores rows where that column is NULL.
Any arithmetic operation with NULL (e.g., `5 + NULL`) results in NULL.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following are native features of the Transmission Control Protocol (TCP)?
options: Guaranteed low-latency delivery suitable for live voice streaming.
Flow Control utilizing a sliding window mechanism.

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following testing methodologies are explicitly classified as White-Box (Structural) testing?
[MULTI-CORRECT] If a Machine Learning model is heavily 'Overfitting' the training data, which of the following strategies can be employed to reduce the overfitting?
options: Training the model for a longer duration (more epochs).
Increasing the volume of diverse training data.
Pruning the tree (if using Decision Trees).

text: [MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following Unix/Linux commands are utilized primarily for Process Management and monitoring?
[MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following file access modes in C will successfully create a new file if the specified file does NOT already exist?
[MULTI-CORRECT] Which of the following statements about C++ Templates are TRUE?
options: Templates allow for generic programming, increasing code reusability.
A template can be specialized to provide a specific implementation for a particular data type.
Using templates heavily can lead to 'Code Bloat' (increased executable size).
